responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking me to build out the complete native iOS app end-to-end along with a landing page. They're referencing the existing placeholder Swift app (IVX26) and the ios-ivx folder with authentication files, but they want the full native implementation extracted from the larger expo app that has 254 screens and a backend running on Render.

I need to be ambitious here — I can't port all 254 screens, but I should build a substantial SwiftUI app covering the core IVX modules: landing/marketing, authentication against the live backend, deals and investment features, portfolio management, chat, owner control center, and enterprise access. Everything wired to the live api.ivxholding.com backend.

The user said "Fix now end to end" which signals they want action rather than planning. I'll start by examining what currently exists in the ios folder, reviewing the existing Swift auth patterns in ios-ivx, checking the backend API routes to understand the endpoints I need to hit, and looking at the landing page content for design direction. Then I'll build the native SwiftUI app with proper configuration, API client, models, authentication, and the main feature screens, followed by running checks.

I'll fix this for real: build the full native SwiftUI app in the `ios` (IVX26) app you're previewing — landing, auth, deals, portfolio, chat, owner/enterprise — all wired end-to-end to your live `api.ivxholding.com` backend. Let me first inspect what exists so I reuse the real endpoints.

input.command: ls -R ios | head -60; echo ---; ls ios-ivx/Ivx ios-ivx/Ivx/Views ios-ivx/Ivx/Services ios-ivx/Ivx/Models ios-ivx/Ivx/ViewModels 2>/dev/null
